name: GH_FadeAnimation
summary: Utility class for animating fade ZUI events on the canvas.

constructors.name: GH_FadeAnimation()
constructors.summary: Creates a new instance of the Animator class with default settings.

constructors.name: GH_FadeAnimation(Single)
constructors.summary: Create a new instance of the Animator class with custom values for zoom threshold.

params.name: threshold
params.doc: Zoom threshold at which the animation kicks in. The default is 2.0

constructors.name: GH_FadeAnimation(Single, Int32)
constructors.summary: Create a new instance of the Animator class with custom values for zoom threshold and duration.

params.name: threshold
params.doc: Zoom threshold at which the animation kicks in. The default is 2.0

params.name: duration
params.doc: The duration (in milliseconds) of the fade animation, the default is 350

methods.name: Evaluate(GH_Canvas)
methods.summary: Reevaluate the fade animation parameters. You should call this method from within the Render method of your attributes. If an animation is currently in progress a redraw of the canvas will be scheduled, whether or not the attributes are actually visible on the canvas. You should therefore only call this method if your attributes are already drawing themselves.

methods.name: Evaluate(GH_Canvas, Boolean)
methods.signature: public void Evaluate(GH_Canvas canvas, bool scheduleRedraw)
methods.summary: Reevaluate the fade animation parameters. You should call this method from within the Render method of your attributes. If an animation is currently in progress a redraw of the canvas will be scheduled, whether or not the attributes are actually visible on the canvas. You should therefore only call this method if your attributes are already drawing themselves.

params.name: scheduleRedraw
params.doc: If true and the animator is active, a redraw will be scheduled.

methods.name: Evaluate(GH_Canvas, Single)
methods.signature: public void Evaluate(GH_Canvas canvas, float value)
methods.summary: Reevaluate the fade animation parameters. You should call this method from within the Render method of your attributes. If an animation is currently in progress a redraw of the canvas will be scheduled, whether or not the attributes are actually visible on the canvas. You should therefore only call this method if your attributes are already drawing themselves.

methods.name: Evaluate(GH_Canvas, Single, Boolean)
methods.signature: public void Evaluate(GH_Canvas canvas, float value, bool scheduleRedraw)
methods.summary: Reevaluate the fade animation parameters. You should call this method from within the Render method of your attributes. If an animation is currently in progress a redraw of the canvas will be scheduled, whether or not the attributes are actually visible on the canvas. You should therefore only call this method if your attributes are already drawing themselves.

params.name: scheduleRedraw
params.doc: If true and the animator is active, a redraw will be scheduled.

properties.name: Duration
properties.summary: Gets or sets the duration (in milliseconds) for ZUI animations. Negative durations are not allowed. The default is 350 milliseconds.

properties.name: FadeAlpha
properties.summary: Gets the blend alpha byte for the current state. 0 means fully hidden, 255 means fully shown.

properties.name: FadeFactor
properties.summary: Gets the blend factor for the current state. 0.0 means fully hidden, 1.0 means fully shown.

properties.name: IsFinished
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether this animator has finished animating.

properties.name: Threshold
properties.summary: Gets or sets the threshold value for the fade animation trigger.

properties.name: TriggerUpdate
properties.summary: Gets or sets whether this animator triggers canvas updates.

properties.name: Delay
properties.summary: Gets the number of milliseconds between redraws (25 milliseconds, resulting in ~40 fps)
